title: Explosives Workers, Ordnance Handling Experts, and Blasters
description: Place and detonate explosives to demolish structures or to loosen, remove, or displace earth, rock, or other materials. May perform specialized handling, storage, and accounting procedures.

tasks.text: Examine blast areas to determine amounts and kinds of explosive charges needed and to ensure that safety laws are observed.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Tie specified lengths of delaying fuses into patterns in order to time sequences of explosions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Place safety cones around blast areas to alert other workers of danger zones, and signal workers as necessary to ensure that they clear blast sites prior to explosions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Place explosive charges in holes or other spots; then detonate explosives to demolish structures or to loosen, remove, or displace earth, rock, or other materials.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Insert, pack, and pour explosives, such as dynamite, ammonium nitrate, black powder, or slurries into blast holes; then shovel drill cuttings, admit water into boreholes, and tamp material to compact charges.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Mark patterns, locations, and depths of charge holes for drilling, and issue drilling instructions.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Compile and keep gun and explosives records in compliance with local and federal laws.
tasks.rationale: Routine cognitive or manual task with high vulnerability to automation.

tasks.text: Measure depths of drilled blast holes, using weighted tape measures.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Connect electrical wire to primers, and cover charges or fill blast holes with clay, drill chips, sand, or other material.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Lay primacord between rows of charged blast holes, and tie cord into main lines to form blast patterns.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Assemble and position equipment, explosives, and blasting caps in holes at specified depths, or load perforating guns or torpedoes with explosives.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Verify detonation of charges by observing control panels, or by listening for the sounds of blasts.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Move and store inventories of explosives, loaded perforating guns, and other materials, according to established safety procedures.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Light fuses, drop detonating devices into wells or boreholes, or activate firing devices with plungers, dials, or buttons, in order to set off single or multiple blasts.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Drive trucks to transport explosives and blasting equipment to blasting sites.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Cut specified lengths of primacord and attach primers to cord ends.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Maintain inventory levels, ordering new supplies as necessary.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Repair and service blasting, shooting, and automotive equipment, and electrical wiring and instruments, using hand tools.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Set up and operate short-wave radio or field telephone equipment to transmit and receive blast information.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Insert waterproof sealers, bullets, and/or powder charges into guns, and screw gun ports back into place.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Clean, gauge, and lubricate gun ports.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Lower perforating guns into wells, using hoists; then use measuring devices and instrument panels to position guns in correct positions for taking samples.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Create and lay out designs for drill and blast patterns.
tasks.rationale: Complex problem-solving, strategic leadership, or high-empathy task requiring human judgment.

tasks.text: Document geological formations encountered during work.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Operate machines to flush earth cuttings or to blow dust from holes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Set up and operate equipment such as hoists, jackhammers, and drills, in order to bore charge holes.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.

tasks.text: Signal crane operators to move equipment.
tasks.rationale: Semi-structured task combining routine and non-routine responsibilities.
